test.command: export MILESTONE="m1-context-pack-core"; if [ -n "${MILESTONE:-}" ]; then "/Users/albertonunez/Documents/Windsurf/BurnBar/.factory/library/verify-flow-report-finalization.sh" "$MILESTONE" || exit 1; fi; "/Users/albertonunez/Documents/Windsurf/BurnBar/scripts/test-openburnbar-swift.sh" && "/Users/albertonunez/Documents/Windsurf/BurnBar/scripts/test-openburnbar-app.sh"

blockingIssues.description: Equal-score sessions are ordered with a descending final ID tie-break, which conflicts with the declared ID-ascending policy and can change which sessions survive the 5-session cap or char-budget trimming.
The exporter uses separate per-target renderers instead of a single shared body, so target outputs can drift and the cross-target byte-equivalence contract is not guaranteed.
Claude/Hermes envelopes inject raw content inside `<context_pack>` without XML escaping or CDATA handling, which can invalidate the envelope when session text contains XML-sensitive sequences.
Usage-summary formatting is locale-sensitive because the date formatter does not pin a stable locale/calendar, so exported summaries are not byte-deterministic across hosts.
